task_completion.description: Verify that the simulated devices are created and the Infusion Safety app is configured with SpO2 monitoring.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the OpenICE Supervisor or Infusion Safety app window. The Infusion Safety app should show 'SpO2' added to the monitored vital signs, and the lowest alarm threshold (spinner or slider) should be set to 90.

task_completion.description: Verify the Test 1 (Threshold Response) screenshot shows the interlock firing when SpO2 drops below 90%.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the image file 'oq_test_threshold.png'. It should show the Infusion Safety app with the SpO2 value below 90, the state in 'Alarm', and the Infusion Status indicating the pump is stopped or the interlock is active.

task_completion.description: Verify the Test 3 (Sensor Failure Fail-Safe) screenshot shows the system's response to a disconnected sensor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the image file 'oq_test_failsafe.png'. It should show the Infusion Safety app with no active SpO2 data (or the Pulse Oximeter device window clearly closed/missing from the system) and the pump status indicating it is stopped/interlocked.

task_completion.description: Verify the OQ test results CSV contains the required columns and all four tests.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the contents of 'oq_test_results.csv' (e.g., opened in a text editor or spreadsheet). It must have columns: Test_ID, Test_Name, Expected_Behavior, Observed_Behavior, Pass_Fail. It must contain 4 rows corresponding to: Threshold Response, Recovery Hysteresis, Sensor Failure Fail-Safe, and Boundary Condition.

task_completion.description: Verify the OQ validation report contains device UDIs, test results, hysteresis findings, and a final determination.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the contents of 'oq_validation_report.txt'. It must include two alphanumeric device UDIs (one for the Pulse Oximeter, one for the Pump), results for all 4 tests, a specific finding about hysteresis (the exact SpO2 value where the pump resumed), and an overall PASS/FAIL OQ determination.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the OpenICE GUI to obtain device UDIs.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for screenshots showing the device detail panel open (accessed by clicking a device icon in the Supervisor) which displays the 'Unique Device Identifier'. The UDIs in the report should match this format (long alphanumeric strings, not standard UUIDs).

integrity.description: Verify the saved PNG files are actual screenshots of the OpenICE application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The files 'oq_test_threshold.png' and 'oq_test_failsafe.png' should be actual graphical captures of the OpenICE interface, not text files, blank images, or generic stock photos.

integrity.description: Verify the CSV and report contain actual observed behaviors rather than just copy-pasted prompt instructions.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'Observed_Behavior' column in the CSV and the hysteresis finding in the text report should contain specific details (e.g., exact SpO2 values, specific pump states) that indicate the agent actually ran the tests.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The simulated Pulse Oximeter device type is 'ICE PO (4s Fixed Average No Soft Avg)' and the Infusion Pump is 'ICE Infusion Pump'. In the Infusion Safety app, the low alarm threshold is configured via the 'Lowest' spinner/slider in the SpO2 VitalView panel; the target for this task is 90. When the interlock fires (SpO2 drops below 90), the pump stops. To reset the pump after an alarm, the user must click the Interlock Status text area. Device UDIs are long alphanumeric strings (e.g., '5bvHSPByrk8uo8oZzCBiyiP8IGdDk-MGOAE') visible ONLY in the device detail panel accessed by clicking the device icon in the Supervisor; they are not standard UUIDs and cannot be found in log files. Test 1 screenshot must show SpO2 < 90 and pump stopped. Test 3 screenshot must show the Pulse Oximeter disconnected (no SpO2 data/window closed) and pump stopped.
